zh.Saint-Blaise-la-Roche, Bas-Rhin, Grand E: 法國德 艾斯特巴-爾沙吉布萊-拉-洛
zh.Plaine,67420,Bas-Rhin,Grand Est,FRANCE,: 法國阿爾薩斯巴-爾普萊

zh.Saint-Blaise-la-Roche, Bas-Rhin, France: 法國巴-爾沙吉布萊-拉-洛
zh.lifeSummary: Mougeotte LE BETZ於大約1627年2月出生在法國巴-爾沙吉布萊-拉-洛。她於大約1645年10月與Nicolas Grandadam dit l'Homme在法國阿爾薩斯巴-爾普萊結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1706年3月1日在法國巴-爾普萊享年79歲，埋葬在法國巴-爾普萊。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mougeotte LE BETZ于大约1627年2月出生在法国巴-爾沙吉布萊-拉-洛。她于大约1645年10月与Nicolas Grandadam dit l'Homme在法国阿爾薩斯巴-爾普萊结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1706年3月1日在法国巴-爾普萊享年79岁，埋葬在法国巴-爾普萊。

nameMeanings.name: LE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: BETZ
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: South German: from a pet form of the personal names   Berthold   or   Bernhard  .
Germanized or Americanized form of Slovenian and Polish   Bec  .

variants.name: Bec
meanings: Slovenian: nickname from an archaic name for a small (Venetian) coin. Compare   Betz  .
Polish: unexplained.
French: nickname from bec ‘beak’, probably denoting a garrulous individual or someone with a prominent nose.

variants.name: Baetz
meanings: German (Bätz): variant of Betz . Compare Batz .

variants.name: Betzer
meanings: German: variant of   Betz  .

variants.name: Betzel
meanings: South German: from a pet form of the personal name   Betz  .

variants.name: Beetz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Hans, Juergen.
variant of   Betz  .
habitational name from a place so named on the Havel river, northwest of Berlin.

variants.name: Batz
meanings: South German: from Alemannic, Swabian Batz ‘pile, large quantity’, possibly applied as a nickname either for a man of large physical proportions or for a man of wealth. The term also denoted a coin and may have been used metonymically for a coiner. The name of the coin is believed to have been derived from its imprint of a bear, known in folklore as Betz or Petz.
South German (Bätz): variant of Betz . Compare Baetz .
Germanized or Americanized form of Polish and Slovenian   Bac  .

variants.name: Berthold
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Hans, Juergen, Claus, Fritz, Gerhard, Goetz, Guenter, Helmut, Merwin, Otto, Siegried.

German and French (mainly Alsace, Doubs, and Lorraine): from the ancient Germanic personal name Bertwald, composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + wald ‘rule’. The second element has been altered by association with German hold ‘lovely, splendid’. This surname is also found in Haiti, where it is very rare.

historicEvents.title: The Palace of Versailles
historicEvents.description: The Palace of Versailles was originally known as a hunting log and retreat for Louis XII. When Louis XIV took over he moved his family into it. After he died the palace was abandoned. It has since been turned into a museum.

historicEvents.title: French Monarchy
historicEvents.description: The French monarchy started way before the 1700s, but for the most part the major events of history took place during this time before the French Revolution started. This was known as the Bourbon Dynasty. Louis XIV the Great, Louis XV the Beloved, Louis XVI the Restorer of French Liberty, and Louis XVII all reigned during this time.

en.lifeSummary: Mougeotte LE BETZ was born about February 1627, in Saint-Blaise-la-Roche, Bas-Rhin, France. She married Nicolas Grandadam dit l'Homme about October 1645, in Plaine, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, France. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 1 March 1706, in Plaine, Bas-Rhin, France, at the age of 79, and was buried in Plaine, Bas-Rhin, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Mougeotte LE BETZ est née environ février 1627, à Saint-Blaise-la-Roche, Bas-Rhin, France. Elle a épousé Nicolas Grandadam dit l'Homme environ octobre 1645, à Plaine, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, France. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 mars 1706, à Plaine, Bas-Rhin, France, à l’âge de 79 ans, et a été enterrée à Plaine, Bas-Rhin, France.

de.lifeSummary: Mougeotte LE BETZ wurde um Februar 1627 in Sankt Blaise, Bas-Rhin, Frankreich geboren. Sie hat um Oktober 1645 in Blen, Niederrhein, Elsaß, Frankreich Nicolas Grandadam dit l'Homme geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 1. März 1706 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Blen, Bas-Rhin, Frankreich und wurde in Blen, Bas-Rhin, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Mougeotte LE BETZ è nata il circa febbraio 1627 nel Saint-Blaise-la-Roche, Bas-Rhin, Francia. Lei sposato Nicolas Grandadam dit l'Homme circa ottobre 1645, nel Plaine, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 1 marzo 1706, nel Plaine, Bas-Rhin, Francia, all'età di 79 anni, e fu sepolta a Plaine, Bas-Rhin, Francia.

ja.Saint-Blaise-la-Roche, Bas-Rhin, France: フランスバス-ルヒンサイント-ブレイセ-ラ-ロチェ
ja.lifeSummary: Mougeotte LE BETZは約1627年2月にフランスバス-ルヒンサイント-ブレイセ-ラ-ロチェで生まれた。約1645年10月にフランスアルサスバス-ルヒンプレアインでNicolas Grandadam dit l'Hommeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1706年3月1日にフランスバス-ルヒンプレアインで79歳で死去し，フランスバス-ルヒンプレアインにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mougeotte LE BETZ(은)는 대략 1627년 2월 프랑스 바스-히 세인트-블레이세-라-록헤에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1645년 10월 프랑스 알사스 바스-히 플레이인에서 Nicolas Grandadam dit l'Homme(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1706년 3월 1일 프랑스 바스-히 플레이인에서 79세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 바스-히 플레이인에 묻혔다.

pt.about February 1627–1 March 1706: aproximadamente fevereiro de 1627–1 de março de 1706
pt.lifeSummary: Mougeotte LE BETZ nasceu aproximadamente fevereiro de 1627, em Saint-Blaise-la-Roche, Bas-Rhin, França. Ela casou-se com Nicolas Grandadam dit l'Homme aproximadamente outubro de 1645, em Plaine, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1 de março de 1706, em Plaine, Bas-Rhin, França, com 79 anos, e foi sepultada em Plaine, Bas-Rhin, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Mougeotte LE BETZ родилась около февраль 1627 г. в местности сеинт-блеисе-ла-рош, бас-рин, Франция. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Nicolas Grandadam dit l'Homme около октябрь 1645 г. в местности плеин, бас-рин, алсеис, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла 1 марта 1706 г. в местности плеин, бас-рин, Франция в возрасте 79 лет и была похоронена в местности плеин, бас-рин, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Mougeotte LE BETZ nació aproximadamente febrero de 1627, en Saint-Blaise-la-Roche, Bas-Rhin, Francia. Se casó con Nicolas Grandadam dit l'Homme aproximadamente octubre de 1645, en Plaine, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 1 de marzo de 1706, en Plaine, Bas-Rhin, Francia, a la edad de 79 años, y fue sepultada en Plaine, Bas-Rhin, Francia.
